ranked_movies.title: Airplane!
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively discussed and praised in the David Zucker AMA as a foundational comedy classic. Frequently cited as one of the funniest movies ever made and a masterclass in spoof comedy.

ranked_movies.title: The Naked Gun
ranked_movies.reason: A central topic of the David Zucker AMA. The unmade script was shared, and the films are celebrated as pinnacles of the spoof genre, leading to discussions about a new installment.

ranked_movies.title: Top Secret!
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended by users in the David Zucker AMA. Praised for its unique blend of spy spoof and musical comedy, with specific questions about Val Kilmer's performance.

ranked_movies.title: Rush Hour
ranked_movies.reason: The primary example used in the post about interracial comedy movies. Discussed for its successful formula and cultural impact, with users debating potential reboots.

ranked_movies.title: Harold & Kumar Go to White Castle
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned alongside Rush Hour as a defining interracial comedy franchise. Users expressed a desire for more sequels and praised its influence.

ranked_movies.title: Scary Movie 3
ranked_movies.reason: Specifically highlighted in the David Zucker AMA as a fan favorite of the franchise that Zucker directed after the Wayans brothers left.

ranked_movies.title: This Is Spinal Tap
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times as the quintessential mockumentary about a 'talentless' band. A cornerstone of the comedy genre.

ranked_movies.title: Shaun of the Dead
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently recommended in comedy suggestion threads. Praised for its perfect blend of horror and comedy (horror-comedy).

ranked_movies.title: Hot Fuzz
ranked_movies.reason: Like Shaun of the Dead, this was highly recommended by multiple users for its action-comedy brilliance and clever writing.

ranked_movies.title: Young Frankenstein
ranked_movies.reason: A top recommendation in the comedy suggestion thread, cited as a masterpiece of parody and Mel Brooks' finest work.

ranked_movies.title: Walk Hard: The Dewey Cox Story
ranked_movies.reason: The original post seeking movies about 'bad' musicians used this as a key example, and it was reaffirmed by others in the comments.

ranked_movies.title: Bhool Bhulaiyaa
ranked_movies.reason: A major hit from the discussed 'greatest year in Bollywood' (2007). Listed as a peak comedy for Akshay Kumar and a career-defining film for Vidya Balan.

ranked_movies.title: Hera Pheri
ranked_movies.reason: The top recommendation in the 'favorite Hindi comedy' thread. Users posted memes and images from the film, cementing its iconic status.

ranked_movies.title: What We Do in the Shadows
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended for both the film and the TV series. Noted for its unique vampire mockumentary style and hilarious execution.

ranked_movies.title: The Big Lebowski
ranked_movies.reason: While not explicitly listed in the provided discussions, it is the quintessential Coen Brothers comedy. The post arguing that every Coen film is a comedy inherently includes this classic.

ranked_movies.title: White Chicks
ranked_movies.reason: Defended by Marlon Wayans and users in the thread about his new film. Cited as a classic that was panned by critics but loved by audiences, proving his point about critic-proof comedies.

ranked_movies.title: Extreme Job
ranked_movies.reason: The most recommended Korean comedy movie. Praised for focusing on pure comedy and being the second highest-grossing film in Korea.

ranked_movies.title: Planes, Trains and Automobiles
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in the thread about comedies that bring you to tears. A classic holiday comedy known for its heart and humor.

ranked_movies.title: Popstar: Never Stop Never Stopping
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in the mockumentary thread as a brilliant and underrated modern spoof of the music industry.

ranked_movies.title: BASEketball
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned by David Zucker in his AMA intro and discussed by fans in the comments as a cult classic from his filmography.
